seoTitle: Mount Cooper vs Mount Crawford — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Mount Cooper and Mount Crawford. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: Mount Cooper vs Mount Crawford: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Mount Cooper and Mount Crawford.
heading: Mount Cooper vs Mount Crawford — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Mount Cooper, South Australia and Mount Crawford, South Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Mount Crawford has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to Mount Cooper (43/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Mount Cooper or Mount Crawford safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Mount Cooper has a flood risk score of 57/100, while Mount Crawford scores 36/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Mount Cooper scores 45/100 for bushfire risk. Mount Crawford scores 33/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Mount Cooper or Mount Crawford?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Mount Crawford has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.9% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
